description: Public-safe policy registry rows derived from InstituteOS policies.

records.description: General accessibility policy covering geographic, financial, cultural, and knowledge access. Replaces the general aspects of the old DEI policy.
records.title: Accessibility Policy

records.description: Social responsibility commitments — including open-access knowledge production, free educational resources, community-driven governance, and contributions to the global scientific commons through Active Inference research
records.title: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Policy

records.description: Environmental commitments for Institute operations — including digital-first operations to minimize physical footprint, sustainable conference practices, and responsible technology procurement aligned with ESG values
records.title: Environmental Sustainability Policy

records.description: Human rights commitments in all Institute activities — including responsible AI/ML research principles, equitable access to educational resources, and respect for research participant rights and dignity
records.title: Human Rights Policy

records.description: Prevention, detection, and response to fraud across Institute operations — including grant fund misuse, expense fraud, research data fabrication, and misrepresentation of organizational status or tax-exempt purpose
records.title: Anti-Fraud Policy

records.description: Annual budget planning cycles, program-level budgeting (Administrative, EduActive, ReInference), grant-funded project budgets, variance tracking, and Board approval of annual financial plans
records.title: Budgeting & Forecasting Policy

records.description: Reimbursable expenses for Board members, Officers, Research Fellows, Interns, and volunteers — including travel for global Board meetings, conference attendance, research supplies, and technology costs for remote participants
records.title: Expense Reimbursement Policy

records.description: Segregation of duties, approval workflows, annual audits, and financial reporting for a 501(c)(3) organization — including grant fund management, philanthropic donations, and expense oversight by the Treasurer (President Daniel Friedman)
records.title: Financial Controls Policy

records.description: Formalized grant procedures covering opportunity identification, alignment screening, capacity evaluation (Red/Yellow/Green framework), conflict-of-interest disclosure and recusal, Officer collaboration workflow, and Board escalation thresholds
records.title: Grant Procedure Policy

records.description: How Institute reserve funds and endowment may be invested, risk tolerance aligned with mission, ethical investment criteria consistent with open-science and Active Inference values, and Board approval requirements
records.title: Investment Policy

records.description: Meta-policy defining how all AII governance artifacts are developed, structured, tiered, monitored, and revised
records.title: Active Inference Policy Development & Monitoring Standard (Meta Policy)

records.description: Meta-policy defining how all other policies are developed, structured, and governed
records.title: Active Inference Policy Development Standard

records.description: Instructions for AI-assisted policy drafting
records.title: AI Policy Instructions

records.description: Prevention of bribery and corruption in all Institute operations, especially relevant given the global Board of Directors, international research collaborations, and partnerships across multiple jurisdictions
records.title: Anti-Bribery & Anti-Corruption Policy

records.description: Corporate bylaws adopted December 12, 2025, defining the governance structure of the Active Inference Institute — Members, Board of Directors, Officers, Committees, compensation, and operational procedures under Delaware nonprofit corporation law
records.title: Bylaws of Active Inference Institute, Inc.

records.description: Expectations for integrity, ethical conduct, conflicts of interest, and professional behavior for all Institute participants — Board members, Officers, Research Fellows, Interns, volunteers, and community contributors engaged in open-science Active Inference research and education
records.title: Code of Conduct / Code of Ethics

records.description: How conflicts of interest are disclosed, reviewed, and managed for Board of Directors, Officers, Scientific Advisory Board members, Research Fellows, and staff — including dual affiliations with academic institutions, partner organizations (e.g. Lazy Dynamics / RxInfer), and grant-funded projects
records.title: Conflict of Interest Policy

records.description: Board of Directors roles, decision-making authority, committee structure (Scientific Advisory Board, Officers), quarterly meeting cadence, fiduciary duties, and oversight of the three organizational units: Administrative, EduActive, and ReInference
records.title: Corporate Governance Policy

records.description: Standardized template for all new policies
records.title: Policy Template

records.description: Template for artifact tiering recommendations
records.title: Tiering Recommendation Packet Template

records.description: Safe, confidential reporting of unethical or illegal conduct within the Institute, covering research misconduct, financial irregularities, and policy violations — required under Delaware nonprofit law and IRS 501(c)(3) best practices
records.title: Whistleblower Policy

records.description: Prevention and response to harassment and discrimination across all Institute channels — including Coda shared space, Discord, YouTube livestreams, research collaborations, and in-person events. California-compliant
records.title: Anti-Harassment & Anti-Discrimination Policy

records.description: Pay structure, stipends for Research Fellows, benefits for staff, and non-monetary benefits (professional development, conference access, publication support) for a mission-driven 501(c)(3) nonprofit
records.title: Compensation & Benefits Policy

records.description: Progressive disciplinary procedures and engagement termination processes for staff, Fellows, Interns, and volunteers — including grounds related to research misconduct, code of conduct violations, and policy breaches
records.title: Disciplinary & Termination Policy

records.description: Umbrella document covering HR policies for Institute staff, Officers, and engagement terms for Research Fellows, Interns, and volunteers — adapted for a globally-distributed, primarily remote open-science organization
records.title: Employee Handbook

records.description: Equal opportunity practices for hiring, engagement, and advancement of all Institute personnel — reflecting commitment to diversity across academic disciplines, geographic regions, career stages, and backgrounds in Active Inference research
records.title: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

records.description: Reviews, feedback, and performance assessment for Officers, staff, Research Fellows, and Interns — incorporating contribution metrics across research output, educational delivery, and community engagement
records.title: Performance Management Policy

records.description: Guidelines for the Institute's remote-first, globally-distributed operations — covering time zone coordination, asynchronous communication norms, equipment support, and hybrid arrangements for California-based personnel
records.title: Remote Work / Hybrid Work Policy

records.description: Learning and development programs for Institute participants — including Active Inference curriculum mastery, open-science research methods, scientific writing, and educational content development skills
records.title: Training & Professional Development Policy

records.description: Health and safety standards for Institute activities — primarily addressing remote work ergonomics, in-person conference/symposium safety, and mental wellness support for distributed research teams
records.title: Workplace Health & Safety Policy

records.description: Acceptable use rules for Institute technology resources — including GitHub repositories, Coda shared space, Discord server, YouTube channel management, cloud computing, and shared research infrastructure
records.title: Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)

records.description: User access provisioning and identity management across Institute platforms — GitHub org roles, Coda shared space permissions, YouTube channel management, cloud services, and administrative systems. Role-based access aligned to organizational units
records.title: Access Control & Identity Management Policy

records.description: Rules for using personal devices for Institute work — relevant for globally-distributed volunteers, Research Fellows, Interns, and Board members who access Institute resources from personal hardware
records.title: Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy

records.description: Ensuring continuity of Institute research programs, educational activities, and community engagement during disruptions — covering technology failures, leadership transitions, and funding interruptions
records.title: Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery Policy

records.description: Overall cybersecurity posture for Institute digital infrastructure — covering GitHub organization security, YouTube channel protection, Coda shared space access, cloud research environments, and website security
records.title: Cybersecurity Policy

records.description: Technical standards for digital accessibility, including WCAG compliance, captioning, and platform-specific requirements. Replaces the technical aspects of the old DEI policy.
records.title: Digital Accessibility Technical Policy

records.description: Incident handling and breach notification procedures for cybersecurity events affecting Institute systems, research data, or community member information — including California breach notification requirements
records.title: Incident Response & Breach Notification Policy

records.description: Data handling, access controls, encryption, incident response. Internal
records.title: Data Protection & Information Security Policy

records.description: Preserving documents, communications, and digital records when legal action is anticipated — covering research records, Board communications, financial data, and contributor agreements
records.title: Litigation Hold Policy

records.description: How the Institute collects, uses, stores, and shares personal data from participants, community members, YouTube viewers, Substack subscribers, and research subjects — GDPR-aware for international participants and CCPA-compliant for California operations
records.title: Privacy Policy

records.description: Retention schedules and secure destruction procedures for research data, educational materials, financial records, Board minutes, grant documentation, and IRS 501(c)(3) compliance records
records.title: Records Retention & Destruction Policy

records.description: Compliance with Delaware nonprofit corporation law, California nonprofit regulations, IRS 501(c)(3) requirements (Form 990, public disclosure), export controls for international research collaborations, and applicable data protection regulations
records.title: Regulatory Compliance Policy

records.description: Identification, assessment, mitigation, and reporting of risks across all Institute programs — including research integrity risks, funding dependency, cybersecurity, reputational risks, and regulatory compliance for multi-jurisdictional operations
records.title: Risk Management Policy

records.description: Handling feedback, concerns, and complaints from community members, research participants, students, and partner organizations — escalation paths from operational staff to Officers to Board of Directors
records.title: Customer Complaints & Escalation Policy

records.description: Document lifecycle management for Institute records — including Board minutes, research papers, grant applications, educational materials, policy documents, and version control practices using GitHub and Coda
records.title: Document Management Policy

records.description: Ownership and licensing of work product created by or for the Institute — defaulting to open-source and Creative Commons licensing for research outputs, educational materials, and software tools consistent with the open-science mission
records.title: Intellectual Property Policy

records.description: Standard operating procedures for core Institute workflows — including livestream production, research project lifecycle, Fellowship onboarding, publication pipeline, and community event coordination
records.title: Operational Procedures / SOPs

records.description: Quality standards for Institute outputs — including research publications, educational materials, course content, YouTube video production, Active Inference Journal submissions, and open-source software contributions
records.title: Quality Assurance Policy

records.description: Authority to sign contracts by role (President, VP, Officers), contract review and legal requirements, and secure storage of partnership agreements, grant contracts, contributor agreements, and vendor contracts
records.title: Contract Approval & Management Policy

records.description: Purchasing thresholds and approvals for Institute operations — including software subscriptions, research tools, educational platform costs, video production equipment, and contracted services. Aligned with 501(c)(3) duty of care
records.title: Procurement Policy

records.description: Ongoing monitoring of vendors, partners, and service providers involved in Institute programs — including cloud infrastructure providers, video hosting, payment processors, and international research collaborators
records.title: Third-Party Risk Management Policy

records.description: Risk, compliance, financial stability, and values-alignment checks for vendors and partners — including technology providers, research collaborators (e.g. Lazy Dynamics/RxInfer), educational platform partners, and contracted consultants
records.title: Vendor Due Diligence Policy

records.description: Proper use of Active Inference Institute brand assets — including logo, name, tagline, color palette, and typography across publications, presentations, partner materials, and merchandise
records.title: Brand Usage Guidelines

records.description: How participant, contributor, and partner data is collected, stored, processed, and protected across Institute platforms — including research subject data, course enrollment, and YouTube analytics
records.title: Client Data Handling Policy

records.description: Guidelines for Institute communications across channels — website (activeinference.institute), Substack newsletter, YouTube, Discord, academic conferences, and media inquiries. Consistent messaging of open-science mission
records.title: Marketing & Communications Policy

records.description: Ethical standards for fundraising, grant applications, partnership solicitations, and philanthropy — ensuring truthful representation of Institute mission, impact, and tax-exempt status in all donor communications
records.title: Sales Ethics Policy

records.description: Social media usage guidelines for official Institute accounts and personnel representing the Institute — covering YouTube (primary channel with 600+ videos), Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Discord, and community forums
records.title: Social Media Policy
